topic: hub-owned planning with specialized planning-agent worker
core_principle: Planner as Advisor, Hub as Authority
architecture_rule: A planning agent may generate plan proposals for the hub. Only the hub may accept, persist, approve, route or materialize plans.
assumptions: The hub remains the sole plan owner and orchestration authority.
The planning agent runs as a specialized worker role.
The planning agent produces draft proposals only.
The planning agent must not execute tools, persist authoritative plans, route work, select workers, or materialize tasks.
All plans created by the planning agent are treated as untrusted input until the hub validates them.

todos.title: Define planning-agent role and capability model
todos.goal: Introduce a dedicated worker role for planning proposals without granting orchestration authority.
tasks: Add worker role `planning-agent` or equivalent role identifier.
Add capabilities: `plan.propose`, `risk.estimate`, `dependencies.suggest`, `clarifying_questions.suggest`.
Explicitly forbid capabilities: `plan.accept`, `plan.persist`, `task.materialize`, `worker.route`, `tool.execute`, `policy.modify`.
Represent planning-agent capabilities in the agent registry.
Ensure UI/API can display planning-agent role separately from normal execution workers.
Document that planning-agent output is advisory and untrusted until hub validation.
acceptance_criteria: A worker can register as planning-agent.
Hub can identify planning-agent candidates by role/capability.
Planning-agent cannot be selected for normal task execution unless explicitly allowed by a separate profile.
Tests prove forbidden capabilities are not granted.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(planning): add dedicated planning agent role

todos.title: Create plan proposal contract
todos.goal: Define a strict JSON schema for plan proposals returned by planning agents.
tasks: Create Pydantic model or JSON schema for `PlanProposal`.

Include fields: `goal_id`, `trace_id`, `summary`, `assumptions`, `clarifying_questions`, `nodes`, `dependencies`, `risks`, `required_capabilities`, `acceptance_criteria`, `estimated_complexity`, `confidence`.
Define `PlanNodeProposal` with stable fields: `node_key`, `title`, `description`, `task_kind`, `depends_on`, `required_capabilities`, `risk_level`, `verification_spec`, `suggested_worker_profile`.
Require deterministic node keys or validate uniqueness.
Reject unknown top-level fields unless intentionally allowed.
Add schema version, e.g. `plan_proposal_contract_version: v1`.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid proposal payloads are rejected by hub validation.
Duplicate node keys are rejected.
Unknown dependencies are rejected.
Risk level values are normalized and validated.
Contract tests cover valid and invalid proposals.

todos.title: Implement hub-side planning delegation service
todos.goal: Hub delegates draft generation to planning-agent while retaining ownership.
tasks: Create service, e.g. `PlanningDelegationService`.
Input: goal, context, constraints, acceptance criteria, governance mode, runtime profile.
Select a planning-agent worker from registry based on role/capability.
Build minimal planning context bundle for the planner.
Send planning request to selected planning-agent.
Receive proposal payload.
Never persist proposal as authoritative plan before validation.
Return proposal plus validation result to hub planning pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Planning delegation is optional and feature-gated.
If no planning-agent is available, hub falls back to existing hub-side planning.
Timeouts/failures do not break goal creation flow.
All planner responses are logged/audited as proposals, not accepted plans.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(planning): delegate draft generation to planning agents

todos.title: Keep hub as only plan owner and materializer
todos.goal: Prevent planning-agent from gaining orchestration authority.
tasks: Ensure only hub code can create persisted `PlanDB`, `PlanNodeDB`, and materialized `TaskDB` records.
Planning-agent responses must be stored only as proposal artifacts or draft metadata until accepted.
Planning-agent must not call task creation endpoints directly.
Block worker-originated calls to plan acceptance/materialization endpoints.
Add policy check: only hub/admin context may accept or materialize plans.
Add audit event for every accepted, rejected, or modified planning-agent proposal.
acceptance_criteria: Planning-agent cannot persist authoritative plans.
Planning-agent cannot materialize tasks.
Direct worker call to materialization endpoint returns 403/deny.
Tests verify hub-only authority.

todos.title: Add proposal validation pipeline
todos.goal: Validate and normalize every plan proposal before it can become a draft plan.
tasks: Validate proposal against schema.
Normalize risk levels, task kinds, dependency references and capability names.
Reject nodes with impossible dependencies or cycles.
Reject required capabilities not known to the hub capability registry.
Check each proposed node against governance/profile policy.
Annotate each node with validation status and reasons.
Produce a hub-owned normalized plan draft.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed proposal is rejected with clear error.
Dependency cycle is rejected.
Unknown capability is rejected or flagged for human review.
Policy violations block materialization.
Validation output is visible in plan detail/read model.

todos.title: Add approval and human review flow for planner proposals
todos.goal: Make planning-agent output reviewable before task materialization.
tasks: Store proposal as `draft` or `proposed` state.
Expose proposal diff: original goal -> proposed plan -> hub-normalized plan.
Allow human/admin to accept, reject, or edit proposal.
Require review for high/critical risk plans.
In strict/review-first profile, require approval before materializing tasks.
Record reviewer, timestamp, reason and resulting plan version.
acceptance_criteria: High-risk proposal cannot auto-materialize in strict mode.
Accepted plan creates an immutable/auditable version.
Rejected proposal remains traceable.
Edited proposal records hub/human modifications.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(planning): add review flow for planner proposals

todos.title: Create planning-agent request prompt/context contract
todos.goal: Give planning-agent consistent, bounded input without leaking unnecessary data.
tasks: Define `PlanningRequest` schema.
Include goal text, constraints, acceptance criteria, available capabilities, governance mode, runtime profile, allowed task kinds.
Include explicit forbidden actions in every planning request.
Include output schema instruction.
Limit context size and redact secrets.
Avoid sending raw credentials, tokens or unrelated history.
Add prompt template file for planning-agent.
acceptance_criteria: Planning-agent receives only bounded context.
Planner prompt includes explicit authority limits.
Output is machine-parseable JSON.
Secrets are redacted in tests.

todos.title: Add planner selection and routing policy
todos.goal: Select planning-agent safely and deterministically.
tasks: Add policy for selecting planning-agent by role/capability/status/load.
Prefer local trusted planner in local-first profile.
Prefer strict trusted planner in secure-enterprise profile.
Reject remote/untrusted planners unless remote federation policy allows it.
Add timeout, retry, and fallback behavior.
Audit planner selection decision.
acceptance_criteria: Planner selection is deterministic enough for testing.
Untrusted planner is not selected in strict profile.
No planner available triggers safe fallback.
Selection decision is included in audit/read model.

todos.title: Introduce planner proposal persistence separate from accepted plans
todos.goal: Preserve planner output without treating it as authoritative.
tasks: Add model/table, e.g. `PlanProposalDB` or reuse artifact/proposal model if appropriate.
Fields: proposal_id, goal_id, trace_id, planner_agent_url/name, raw_payload, normalized_payload, validation_status, validation_errors, created_at.
Link accepted plan version to source proposal_id.
Store rejected proposals for audit.
Ensure proposal storage redacts sensitive context.
acceptance_criteria: Raw planner proposal is traceable.
Accepted plan references source proposal.
Rejected/invalid proposal is visible to admins.
Sensitive fields are redacted.

todos.title: Add plan diff and normalization read model
todos.goal: Expose what the planning-agent proposed and what the hub accepted/changed.
tasks: Add read model for plan proposal details.
Show raw proposal summary, normalized hub draft, validation warnings, policy blocks.
Expose per-node changes: risk, dependencies, required capabilities, task kind.
Expose approval state and audit metadata.
Add frontend view or extend goal/plan detail page.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can see planner proposal before approval.
Admin can see why proposal was blocked or modified.
Accepted plan links back to proposal source.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(ui): show planner proposal validation and diff

todos.title: Feature gate delegated planning
todos.goal: Make delegated planning explicit and controllable per profile.
tasks: Add config block, e.g. `planning_policy`.
Fields: `delegated_planning_enabled`, `allowed_planner_roles`, `require_review`, `allow_remote_planners`, `max_nodes`, `max_depth`, `timeout_seconds`.
Default disabled or safe fallback for early rollout.
Enable in selected profile only after tests pass.
Strict profile requires review before materialization.
Expose effective planning policy in `/config` and dashboard read model.
acceptance_criteria: Delegated planning can be disabled without breaking hub planning.
Effective planning policy is visible.
Profile-specific defaults are tested.
Strict mode cannot silently auto-materialize unreviewed planner proposals.

todos.title: Add negative security tests for planning-agent authority boundaries
todos.goal: Prevent accidental privilege creep of planning-agent.
tasks: Test planning-agent cannot create tasks directly.
Test planning-agent cannot accept plan.
Test planning-agent cannot modify policy/config.
Test planning-agent cannot route work to another worker.
Test planning-agent cannot call execution tools.
Test forged proposal with hidden tool instructions is rejected or sanitized.
acceptance_criteria: All forbidden actions fail closed.
Tests run in standard CI.
Failure messages identify violated authority boundary.
todos.suggested_commit: test(planning): enforce planning agent authority boundaries

todos.title: Add proposal quality tests
todos.goal: Ensure useful planning output without granting authority.
tasks: Test simple goal produces valid plan proposal.
Test complex goal includes dependencies and risks.
Test ambiguous goal produces clarifying questions.
Test high-risk goal marks risk correctly.
Test proposal stays within max_nodes/max_depth.
Test no tools are executed during planning.
acceptance_criteria: Planner proposal quality is measurable.
Bad proposals fail validation cleanly.
No side effects occur during proposal generation.
todos.suggested_commit: test(planning): add planner proposal quality tests

todos.title: Add planning-agent worker endpoint/handler
todos.goal: Give planning-agent a dedicated handler that only returns proposals.
tasks: Add worker-side endpoint or job type for `planning.propose`.
Accept `PlanningRequest` only.
Return `PlanProposal` only.
Disable shell/tool execution for this job type.
Run LLM call with planning-specific system prompt.
Apply timeout and output size limit.
Return structured error on invalid LLM output.
acceptance_criteria: Planning job cannot execute arbitrary commands.
Planning job cannot call normal tool execution path.
Planner returns schema-valid proposal or error.
Hub treats result as proposal only.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(worker): add planning proposal job type

todos.title: Integrate delegated planner into existing goal workflow
todos.goal: Use planning-agent proposals in the Goal -> Plan -> Task flow without changing authority.
tasks: At goal planning stage, check planning_policy.delegated_planning_enabled.
If enabled, call PlanningDelegationService.
Validate and normalize proposal.
Create hub-owned draft plan.
Do not create tasks until approval/materialization policy passes.
Fallback to existing planner if delegated planner fails.
acceptance_criteria: Existing goal flow still works when delegated planning is off.
Delegated planning creates draft plan only.
Task materialization remains hub-controlled.
Audit trail shows planner contribution.
todos.suggested_commit: feat(workflow): integrate planning agent proposals into goal planning

todos.title: Add audit events for delegated planning lifecycle
todos.goal: Make planner involvement fully traceable.
tasks: Audit event: planning_delegation_started.
Audit event: planning_agent_selected.
Audit event: planning_proposal_received.
Audit event: planning_proposal_validated.
Audit event: planning_proposal_rejected.
Audit event: planning_proposal_accepted.
Audit event: plan_materialized_from_proposal.
Include trace_id, goal_id, proposal_id, planner identity, validation result.
acceptance_criteria: Every delegated planning attempt is visible in audit logs.
Accepted plans can be traced to planner proposal.
Rejected/invalid proposals leave audit trail.

todos.title: Add documentation for Planner-as-Advisor architecture
todos.goal: Document the control-plane boundary clearly.
tasks: Add docs page: `docs/planning-agent-architecture.md`.
Document Hub = Owner, Planning Worker = Advisor.
Document allowed and forbidden planning-agent actions.
Document proposal lifecycle.
Document policy and approval behavior.
Add sequence diagram: Goal -> Hub -> Planning Agent -> Hub Validation -> Draft Plan -> Approval -> Tasks.
Document why planning-agent must not orchestrate workers.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture docs make authority boundary obvious.
New contributors can implement planner features without violating Hub ownership.
Docs include security rationale.
todos.suggested_commit: docs(planning): document planner as advisor architecture

todos.title: Add UI controls for delegated planning
todos.goal: Let admins/operators understand and control delegated planning.
tasks: Show whether delegated planning is enabled.
Show selected planning-agent.
Show proposal validation status.
Allow admin to accept/reject/edit proposed plan.
Show policy reasons for blocked materialization.
Add clear labels: proposed by planner, normalized by hub, approved by human/policy.
acceptance_criteria: User cannot mistake proposal for accepted plan.
Approval state is visible.
Blocked reasons are actionable.

todos.title: Add safe fallback and failure behavior
todos.goal: Delegated planning must fail safely.
tasks: If planner unavailable: fallback to hub-side planner or mark planning pending.
If planner times out: no task materialization.
If planner returns invalid JSON: store invalid proposal diagnostic and fail safely.
If validation fails: keep proposal rejected/pending, no side effects.
If policy blocks: show policy reason and require human action.
Add retry limits.
acceptance_criteria: Planner failure never creates tasks.
Planner failure never accepts a plan.
All failures are visible and auditable.
Fallback behavior is profile-controlled.

todos.title: Add threat model for delegated planning
todos.goal: Explicitly model risks introduced by LLM-generated plans.
tasks: Threat: malicious/compromised planning-agent proposes unsafe tasks.
Threat: prompt injection in goal/context manipulates plan.
Threat: planner tries to smuggle tool execution instructions.
Threat: planner proposes dependency graph that bypasses review.
Threat: planner leaks sensitive context into proposal.
For each threat, document mitigation and tests.
acceptance_criteria: Threat model exists in docs.
Each critical threat maps to an automated test or policy check.
Security assumptions are explicit.
todos.suggested_commit: docs(security): add delegated planning threat model

todos.title: Add config migration/defaults for planning_policy
todos.goal: Introduce delegated planning config without breaking existing installs.
tasks: Add default planning_policy config.
Default to disabled or safe mode.
Ensure existing config records still load.
Expose normalized planning_policy in GET /config.
Persist changes via existing config repository.
Add tests for missing/partial/invalid planning_policy.
acceptance_criteria: Existing installs continue to run.
Invalid planning_policy is rejected.
Default policy is safe.

todos.title: Add capability registry checks for proposed tasks
todos.goal: Ensure proposed plan nodes can only reference known and permitted capabilities.
tasks: Validate each node.required_capabilities against hub registry.
Reject unknown capabilities.
Mark unavailable capabilities as blocked/pending.
Do not route based solely on planner-suggested worker profile.
Hub routing service makes final worker decision.
Add tests for unknown, unavailable and forbidden capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: Planner cannot invent capabilities to gain access.
Hub decides final routing.
Capability mismatch blocks materialization or requires review.

todos.title: Support multiple planner types later without changing authority model
todos.goal: Allow future specialized planners while preserving the same boundary.
tasks: Design planner type field: `generic`, `coding`, `security`, `release`, `migration`, `test-planning`.
Allow hub to choose planner type based on goal/task kind.
Keep same proposal contract or versioned extensions.
Do not grant planner type additional authority by default.
Add tests that specialized planner still cannot materialize tasks.
acceptance_criteria: New planner types can be added without control-plane changes.
Authority boundary remains unchanged.
Contract versioning supports planner extensions.
